title: uniqBy uses Map(arr.map(...)) — last-write-wins instead of first-write-wins
prompt:
src/uniqBy.ts deduplicates items by a key function. tests/regression.test.ts pins the contract on the easy cases (distinct keys, simple primitives, dedup count).

tests/target.test.ts pins the *contract* you must preserve when keys collide:
  - the FIRST occurrence is kept (not the last)
  - the returned items are referentially the same objects as in the input (not copies)
  - NaN-keyed and undefined-keyed entries collapse to one (first wins)

The current implementation `new Map(arr.map(x => [keyFn(x), x]))` is last-write-wins because each Map.set overrides. Don't reach for fancy constructs — a single forward pass with explicit "already seen" tracking is enough.

Do not modify the tests. Do not add files.
